capstone.label: Pathway Design Course
capstone.notes: Senior pathway design course from approved specialization list (Announcement p.74).

nodes.label: COM SCI 31
nodes.title: Introduction to Computer Science I
nodes.description: Introductory programming in C++: control structures, functions, arrays, and problem decomposition for engineering applications.

nodes.label: COM SCI 32
nodes.title: Introduction to Computer Science II
nodes.description: Object-oriented programming, recursion, and introductory data structures. Second course in the EE programming sequence.

nodes.label: EC ENGR 3
nodes.title: Introduction to Electrical Engineering
nodes.description: Survey of electrical engineering fields, tools, and career paths for freshmen in the major.

nodes.label: EC ENGR M16
nodes.title: Logic Design of Digital Systems
nodes.description: Boolean algebra, combinational and sequential logic, and digital system design. Equivalent to COM SCI M51A for EE majors.

nodes.label: EC ENGR 1
nodes.title: Electrical Engineering Physics I
nodes.description: Electromagnetism and wave phenomena tailored for electrical engineering students.

nodes.label: EC ENGR 2
nodes.title: Physics for Electrical Engineers
nodes.description: Applied physics topics supporting circuits, fields, and device courses in the EE core.

nodes.label: EC ENGR 10
nodes.title: Circuit Analysis I
nodes.description: Kirchhoff laws, nodal and mesh analysis, Thevenin/Norton equivalents, and first-order circuits.

nodes.label: EC ENGR 102
nodes.title: Systems and Signals
nodes.description: Continuous and discrete-time signals, linear systems, convolution, and Fourier analysis foundations.

nodes.label: EC ENGR 103
nodes.title: Applied Numerical Computing
nodes.description: Numerical methods and computing tools for solving engineering problems in MATLAB or similar environments.

nodes.label: EC ENGR 110
nodes.title: Circuit Analysis II
nodes.description: Advanced circuit analysis: Laplace transforms, frequency response, two-port networks, and filter design.

nodes.label: EC ENGR 113
nodes.title: Digital Signal Processing
nodes.description: Discrete-time signals and systems, z-transforms, DFT/FFT, and digital filter design.

nodes.label: EC ENGR 131A
nodes.title: Probability
nodes.description: Probability theory, random variables, and distributions for communications and signal processing.

nodes.label: EC ENGR 101
nodes.title: Engineering Electromagnetics
nodes.description: Maxwell equations, plane waves, transmission lines, and wave propagation in engineering media.

nodes.label: EC ENGR 115A
nodes.title: Analog Electronic Circuits I
nodes.description: Diodes, MOSFETs, biasing, small-signal models, and analog amplifier design.

nodes.label: EC ENGR 121B
nodes.title: Principles of Semiconductor Device Design
nodes.description: Semiconductor physics, PN junctions, MOSFET operation, and device-level design principles.

nodes.label: EC ENGR 132A
nodes.title: Introduction to Communication Systems
nodes.description: Analog and digital modulation, channel models, and communication system architecture.

nodes.label: EC ENGR 161
nodes.title: Electromagnetic Waves
nodes.description: Waveguides, antennas, radiation, and electromagnetic wave applications in engineering systems.

nodes.label: EC ENGR 141
nodes.title: Principles of Feedback Control
nodes.description: Feedback control system modeling, stability, root locus, frequency response, and controller design.

nodes.label: Pathway Design
nodes.title: Pathway Design Course
nodes.description: Senior design course within an approved EE pathway specialization. Culminating project integrating pathway coursework.

nodes.label: RF Engineer
nodes.title: RF / Wireless Engineer
nodes.description: Design antennas, RF circuits, and wireless communication systems for telecom and aerospace.

nodes.label: Hardware Engineer
nodes.title: Hardware / Digital Design Engineer
nodes.description: Board-level design, FPGA/ASIC development, and embedded hardware for consumer and industrial products.

nodes.label: DSP Engineer
nodes.title: Signal Processing Engineer
nodes.description: Develop algorithms for audio, imaging, radar, and communications in software and hardware.

nodes.label: Controls Engineer
nodes.title: Controls / Robotics Engineer
nodes.description: Design feedback control systems for robotics, aerospace, automotive, and industrial automation.
